intro: Let's begin. Find a comfortable position, and let your eyes close if that feels right.
outro: Take your time coming back. There's no rush. When you're ready, gently open your eyes.
grounding_breath: Take a slow breath in through your nose, feeling your stomach rise. And let it go, slowly, through your mouth. Again. In. And out.
body_scan_intro: Bring your attention to the top of your head. Slowly let your awareness travel downward, noticing any tension without trying to change it.
releasing_tension: Notice your shoulders. Let them drop, just slightly, away from your ears. Let your jaw soften. Let your hands rest, open and heavy.
racing_thoughts: If your mind is busy, that's alright. You don't need to stop the thoughts. Just notice them, like clouds passing, and gently return to your breath.
counting_breath: Breathe in for a count of four. Hold gently for four. And release for a count of six. We'll repeat this together, slowly.
body_heaviness: Feel the weight of your body where it rests. The chair, or the floor, holding you. You don't need to hold yourself up right now.
single_focus: Choose one point of focus — the feeling of air at your nostrils, or the sound around you. Whenever your mind wanders, just come back to it.
self_compassion: Whatever you're carrying today, it's allowed to be here. You don't need to fix it right now. You're just allowed to rest.
closing_stillness: Stay here a moment longer, in the stillness. There's nowhere else you need to be.
